---
name: Learning Platform - Copilot Instructions
description: |
  Use when implementing specs, fixing bugs, or performing feature work for the Learning Platform.
  The agent should follow the project conventions and consult the repository skills before coding.
  Trigger keywords: "implement a spec", "spec-implementation", "read .copilot/skills", "commit conventions"
applyTo: "**/*"
---

Stack: Next.js 15 App Router, TypeScript (strict), Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, tRPC, Prisma, PostgreSQL

Project structure and absolute rule
- Structure: the app lives in `app/`, specs live in `specs/` (generated by SpecKit)
- Architecture: hexagonal ظ¤ `domain` ظْ `application` ظْ `infrastructure`
- Absolute rule: never import from `infrastructure/` into `domain/`.
- Naming rule: file names, classes, variables, and symbols must remain in English (translations limited to user-facing content).

Commit conventions
- Use `feat|fix|chore` + SpecKit Issue reference. Example: "feat: add auth flow, closes #12".

When asked to implement a spec (required workflow)
1. Always start by reading:
   - specs/[n]-[name]/spec.md
   - specs/[n]-[name]/checklists/plan.md
   - specs/[n]-[name]/checklists/tasks.md
   - .copilot/skills/spec-implementation.md
2. Before each task, read the corresponding skill in `.copilot/skills/`.
3. Never implement multiple tasks at the same time.

Reference of available skills (local path `.copilot/skills`)
| Need                     | Skill to read                        |
|-------------------------|-------------------------------------|
| Database schema          | .copilot/skills/prisma-schema.md    |
| Business rule / use case| .copilot/skills/use-case.md         |
| API Route               | .copilot/skills/trpc-router.md      |
| UI Component            | .copilot/skills/component.md        |
| Design system           | .copilot/skills/design-system.md    |
| Authentication          | .copilot/skills/auth.md             |
| Unit test               | .copilot/skills/test-unit.md        |
| E2E test                | .copilot/skills/test-e2e.md         |
| Implement a spec        | .copilot/skills/spec-implementation.md |

Notes on discovery and best practices
- Place workspace-level instructions in `.github/copilot-instructions.md` or `AGENTS.md` at the repository root so Copilot workspace discovery will pick them up.
- Skills are kept in `.copilot/skills/` in this repository; if you want maximum compatibility with other agents, mirror or copy production-ready skills to `.github/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (optional).
- Ensure every skill file has a clear `description` that includes trigger keywords; the agent uses `description` to decide when to load a skill.
- Avoid overly-broad `applyTo: "**"` unless the instruction truly applies to every request (this file uses `applyTo: "**/*"` to be workspace-wide by design).

If you want, I can also mirror the `.copilot/skills` files into `.github/skills/` so skills are discovered by tools expecting that folder structure.

---

Maintainer: team
